RepresentativeInfo.CapitolAddress: MO House of Representatives, 201 West Capitol Avenue, Room 115-E, Jefferson City MO 65101
RepresentativeInfo.Biography:
Representative Scott Miller, a Republican, represents part of St. Charles County (District 69) in the Missouri House of Representatives. He was elected to his first two-year term in November 2024.
Rep. Miller is a 6th-generation, lifelong resident of Missouri and of St. Charles County for the last 20 years. He is a 1989 graduate of Pattonville Senior High School and continued his education, earning a Bachelor’s degree from the University of Missouri – St. Louis. Additionally, he has earned a Masters of Telecommunications Management from Washington University, MBA and MS in Strategic Management from Indiana University.
Rep. Miller is a proud United States veteran, having served in the United States Army, US Army Reserve, and the Missouri National Guard in Field Artillery and Combat Engineers.
Beyond his legislative duties, Rep. Miller is a small business owner, husband, and father. He is happily married to his amazing wife, Michelle, and has two brilliant daughters, Joanna and Kathryn. He also enjoys RV camping with family and friends and riding motorcycles around the country.
Rep. Miller is passionate about fostering economic growth, supporting small businesses, assuring individual freedom, and ensuring a brighter future for St. Charles County and the entire state of Missouri.

SponsoredBill.LongTitle: Modifies the minimum wage and overtime administrative complaint process, establishing additional requirements for employees filing certain employment complaints
Authorizes exemptions from minimum wage and overtime compensation requirements for certain employees
Modifies provisions governing the seizure of property for tax delinquencies
Adds provisions including gifted education pupil count for purposes of funding schools through the foundation formula
Establishes a quality control committee for oversight and enacts education reforms
Proposes a constitutional amendment to repeal certain constitutional provisions relating to reproductive freedom
Proposes a constitutional amendment that requires the full text of initiative petitions to appear on the ballot and limits initiative petitions to 75 words, including existing language

CoSponsoredBill.LongTitle: Establishes the "STEM Career Awareness Activity Program"
Establishes provisions relating to civil liability for publishing or distributing material harmful to minors on the internet
Allows the court to enter a judgment of dissolution of marriage or legal separation if a person is pregnant
Changes the law regarding firearm concealed carry permits
Modifies provisions relating to motor vehicle assessment valuations
Designates KSHE 95 as the official rock radio station of the state of Missouri
Prohibits public institutions of higher learning from discriminating against religious, political, or ideological associations
Provides protections against discrimination and antisemitism in public schools and public postsecondary educational institutions
Establishes the offense of unlawful tracking of a motor vehicle
Modifies provisions relating to the age of marriage
Modifies provisions governing county sales taxes for park purposes to allow tax revenues to be used for storm water management projects in parks
Establishes the "AI Non-Sentience and Responsibility Act"
Proposes a constitutional amendment relating to property tax exemptions
